THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter launched a new Parks Committee this week with its sights set on a $150,000–$200,000 playground overhaul at Bushka Park. Meanwhile, Wausau school officials and the teachers' union were scheduled to swap opening contract proposals for the 2026–27 school year.

  • Kronenwetter's newly formed Parks Committee elected Erin Mishka as chairperson and Dan Lesniac as vice chairperson at its inaugural June 29 meeting.
  • The Kronenwetter Parks Committee is targeting a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park, with a movie-under-the-stars fundraiser planned for July 17.
  • A public parks tour is set for Wednesday, July 8 at 4 p.m. at the Kronenwetter municipal center, with July 9 as the rain date.
  • The Wausau Board of Education was set to exchange initial written contract proposals with the Wausau Education Association for the 2026–27 school year at a July 1 public session.

Coming up: Monday brings a full slate, including Wausau's Parks & Recreation Committee at 5:15 p.m. and Weston's Village Board of Trustees and Community Life & Public Safety Committee, plus a revised CLIPP Committee meeting in Kronenwetter at 6 p.m. Later in the week, watch for Marathon County's Infrastructure Committee on Thursday and the City of Wausau's Infrastructure & Facilities and Sustainability committees, also Thursday.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
